target.name: SambaNova Systems
target.founder: Rodrigo Liang, Kunle Olukotun, Christopher Ré

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $2.7B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
SambaNova Systems is a Series D chip design company that has raised $1.1B to date against a current post-money valuation of $5.0B. The valuation assessment draws from a universe of 3 private peers in the semiconductors sector at the Series C–D stage (Tenstorrent, d-Matrix, DEEPX) and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $5.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, implies $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage privates yields a median of $2.0B, with a range of $529M to $2.7B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable due to the absence of disclosed target revenue; the sector P/S median of 20.3x across the three public tickers cannot be deployed without this data. Notably, the peer sample shows substantial dispersion, with Tenstorrent valued at $2.7B (Series D) and DEEPX at $529M (Series C).

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $1.7B (low, peer-floor) to $8.6B (high, stage-progression inflection), with a median of $4.8B. The current $5.0B valuation sits near the median but materially above peer-median, reflecting either SambaNova's differentiated position or near-term expectations of significant revenue traction. Key uncertainties include the target's unrevealed revenue base and the wide dispersion among peer valuations in an immature, capital-intensive sector.
